new_classes.definition_en: A process by which a fermented product is purified to a degree that causes it to lose the organoleptic properties and characteristics of a fermented beverage, yielding an undenatured ethyl alcohol of alcoholic strength less than 80 % vol.

new_classes.label_en: undenatured ethyl alcohol < 80 % vol in containers > 2 L (CN 2208 90 99)
new_classes.label_de: unvergällter Ethylalkohol < 80 % vol in Behältnissen > 2 L (KN 2208 90 99)
new_classes.definition_en: Undenatured ethyl alcohol of an alcoholic strength by volume of less than 80 % vol, presented in containers holding more than 2 litres, obtained not exclusively through fermentation but also through a purification process causing the product to lose the properties and characteristics of fermented beverages.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the three main distinguishing criteria found in the legal text: (1) undenatured status (isDenatured hasValue false / complement of DenaturingProcess), (2) alcoholic strength below 80 % vol (decimalRange maxExclusive 80.0), and (3) production via a purification process in addition to fermentation (someValuesFrom PurificationProcess), and (4) container volume > 2 L (someValuesFrom LargeContainer, using existing eucn:LargeContainer). The loss-of-fermented-beverage-characteristics criterion is captured conceptually through the PurificationProcess class definition but cannot be fully formalised as a crisp OWL restriction without a dedicated organoleptic-loss quality individual, which would require additional axioms beyond the current TBox. The 'not exclusively through fermentation' condition is represented by the PurificationProcess involvement but cannot be expressed as a strict owl:complementOf on a pure fermentation process without risking unintended inferences. The existing eucn:OrganolepticCharacteristicsQuality class exists in the TBox but linking it formally to the loss/acquisition transformation is outside OWL-DL expressivity; hence a small coverage gap remains.
